column5: Education is the light that no darkness can extinguish.
column6: When we invest in learning, we build a fire that no ignorance can ever fully blow out.

column5: True education goes beyond textbooks – it nurtures empathy, critical thinking, and the courage to stand up for what is right.
column6: A classroom that only memorises facts graduates workers; one that builds character graduates world‑changers.

column5: A mind fed with knowledge becomes a heart full of compassion.
column6: Understanding the world deeply naturally turns our gaze toward the suffering of others, and that gaze is the beginning of action.

column5: Learning is the seed; peace is the fruit.
column6: Every lesson taught with care is a root that will one day hold a more peaceful world steady against the storms of hatred.

column5: Knowledge shared is knowledge multiplied.
column6: The only resource that grows when you give it away is understanding – share it recklessly.

column5: Educate one child; change a generation.
column6: That child will teach their children, who will teach theirs, and soon a single spark has lit an entire lineage of light.

Heading: World Youth Skills Day
column5: A skill in a young hand is a key to a thousand locked doors.
column6: Today we honour youth skills – because confidence without competence is a bird without wings.

column5: Curiosity is the beginning of wisdom.
column6: A child who asks 'why' is not annoying – they are the only honest adults in the room.

column5: Literacy is freedom written in ink.
column6: To read is to escape any cage; to write is to build the key yourself.

column5: An educated society is a peaceful society.
column6: Fear of the unknown shrinks when knowledge walks into the room, and with it, the excuses for conflict dissolve.

column5: Every teacher plants seeds of the future.
column6: You may never see the fruit, but you will feel the shade of the tree your lessons have grown.

Heading: International Youth Day
column5: Youth is not a waiting room – it is the main hall of change.
column6: The world does not need young people to become leaders tomorrow – it needs their moral clarity today.

column5: The best investment a nation can make is in its schools.
column6: Bomb factories make a country feared; schoolhouses make it loved – and lastingly strong.

column5: Learning never ends, and neither does growing.
column6: The moment you believe you have graduated from life is the moment you volunteer for irrelevance.

column5: Teach a child to question; protect the future of truth.
column6: A generation that does not question authority will inherit every lie it was too tired to challenge.

column5: Language is the bridge; silence is the wall.
column6: When we teach children to speak honestly and listen deeply, we give them the only tools that can dismantle hatred.

Heading: International Literacy Day
column5: To read is to borrow someone else’s eyes; to write is to lend your own.
column6: On Literacy Day we remember: an illiterate person is not unintelligent – they are a library whose lights have been switched off.

column5: Education is the great equalizer.
column6: Birth should not determine destiny – and a good school is the levelest playing field humanity has ever invented.

column5: An open mind is the greatest classroom.
column6: Four walls do not teach; a willingness to be wrong does – and that willingness can live anywhere.

column5: Read widely; judge less.
column6: Every book you open is another prejudice you may close forever.

column5: Every library is a temple of democracy.
column6: A free society rests on free information – and that sanctuary has no membership fee.

Heading: World Teachers’ Day
column5: A teacher’s heart is the only classroom that never locks its doors.
column6: Great teachers do not retire – they simply take their fire to another corner of the world.

column5: Education without values is training without purpose.
column6: A skilled monster is far more dangerous than an ignorant one – values must sit at the head of every class.

Heading: World Students’ Day
column5: A student’s question is more sacred than any answer.
column6: Today we celebrate students – because a room full of question marks is a future full of exclamation points.

column5: True education teaches us to see ourselves in others.
column6: The humanities are not soft – they are the scalpel that cuts away the illusion of otherness.

column5: The classroom that welcomes all voices teaches the greatest lesson.
column6: Inclusion is not charity – it is the only way to hear the full music of humanity before composing tomorrow’s symphony.

column5: Every child who opens a book today opens a door that no dictator can close.
column6: Let the first day of the year be a promise to yourself: you will never stop asking 'why'.

column5: Education is not filling a bucket but lighting a fire.
column6: A bucket of facts empties; a fire of curiosity cooks for a lifetime.

column5: Experience is the hardest teacher – it gives the test first and the lesson after.
column6: But a good classroom can give the lesson first, so the test does not break you.

column5: Grades measure memory, not imagination – and imagination is what changes the world.
column6: Teach for the child who will invent the cure, not just the one who will memorise it.

column5: A home with one book is a palace; a home with none is a prison.
column6: Make your shelf a kingdom of borrowed lives.

column5: The child who asks 'why' ten times is not annoying – they are building a cathedral of understanding.
column6: Each 'why' is a brick; let them build.

column5: The best teachers never stop being students.
column6: A teacher who has forgotten how to learn teaches only the past, not the future.

column5: A conversation on a bus can teach more than a semester of silence.
column6: Listen to the stranger – their life is a textbook you have never read.

column5: Literacy is the paper boat that carries a child across the ocean of poverty.
column6: Once they can read, no wave of ignorance can sink them.

column5: A teacher’s voice is the only thing that grows louder when repeated.
column6: Every lesson echoes in the lives of students you will never meet.

column5: Respect is not taught by lecture – it is caught by watching how you speak to the cleaner.
column6: Your students are always learning from your smallest actions.

column5: Falling in love with learning is the only romance that never ends in heartbreak.
column6: It will stay with you when lovers leave and fortunes fade.

column5: A dropout is not a failure – they are a story that someone stopped reading too soon.
column6: Every adult education class is a promise to finish that story.

column5: Curiosity is the window through which the world enters the soul.
column6: Keep it open, even when the wind of trouble blows.

column5: Mathematics is the alphabet of nature – no translation needed.
column6: A formula written in India works in Iceland; that is the world’s real universal language.

column5: Every time you teach a child to read, you graduate a new citizen of the world.
column6: Their passport is their mind, and no border can stamp it closed.

column5: Failure is the oldest and most honest teacher – she never lies and never flatters.
column6: But she also never leaves you if you are willing to learn from her.

column5: A girl with a notebook is more dangerous to tyranny than a soldier with a gun.
column6: Because ideas cannot be shot – they multiply.

column5: Geography teaches us that borders are lines on paper, but rivers and mountains are real.
column6: Know the difference – and you will know why peace matters.

column5: History is not a list of dates – it is a million stories of people who loved and lost.
column6: Teach those stories, and you teach compassion.

column5: Philosophy does not give answers – it teaches you how to live with better questions.
column6: And a good question is worth a thousand bad answers.

column5: Listening is the first skill of learning, and the last one we master.
column6: Before you can read, you must hear – hear the world, hear others, hear the silence.

column5: Life will educate you whether you sign up or not – the only choice is whether you pay attention.
column6: So pay attention now, before the tuition rises.

column5: A curious mind never runs out of space – every answer writes ten new questions.
column6: That is not frustration; that is the joy of forever learning.

column5: A library book is a gift from a stranger you will never meet – repay them by reading it well.
column6: Then pass the gift on.

column5: Every graduation is a comma, not a full stop.
column6: The sentence of your education continues as long as you breathe.

column5: The hardest lessons are learned in silence – grief, patience, forgiveness.
column6: No textbook can teach them; only life can.

column5: Knowledge hoarded is knowledge lost; knowledge shared is knowledge multiplied.
column6: Write your notes for the next person – they will write theirs for you.

Heading: World Book Day (UNESCO)
column5: To open a book is to borrow someone else’s life for a while – return it richer.
column6: Today, read a book from a country you have never visited – and visit it with your imagination.

column5: A great teacher’s voice never dies – it echoes in the decisions you make forty years later.
column6: Listen carefully – you can still hear them.

column5: Every spring is a reminder that learning, like nature, has its seasons.
column6: Sometimes you need to lie fallow before you can grow again.

column5: Courage is not taught in a lesson plan – it is caught from watching someone stand up when they are afraid.
column6: Be that someone.

column5: There is no deadline on learning – the brain grows as long as the heart beats.
column6: You can start algebra at sixty, and that is not sad – that is magnificent.

column5: When you are lost, education is the compass that points north.
column6: It does not guarantee a straight line, but it guarantees you are moving somewhere true.

column5: The world is a school, and every person you meet is a teacher.
column6: Some teach you patience, some teach you pain – all teach you something.

column5: The quietest child often hears the most – do not mistake shyness for emptiness.
column6: Inside them, a universe is unfolding; give them time to speak.

column5: Sometimes the most important lesson is to unlearn what you were taught.
column6: Prejudice is learned – and it can be unlearned, one honest conversation at a time.

column5: A student’s backpack should be heavy with questions, not just textbooks.
column6: Questions are the fuel of discovery – carry more than answers.

column5: Education is a candle that loses nothing by lighting another.
column6: Light every candle you can – the dark is vast, but it is not infinite.

column5: A teacher writes on hearts, not blackboards – and those marks never erase.
column6: Years later, you will remember the teacher who believed in you.

column5: Life’s exam is open book – but you have to remember where you put the book.
column6: Keep your learning close, and you will never fail.

column5: Learning takes time – a seed does not become a tree in a day.
column6: Be patient with yourself and with every child you teach.

column5: Education is not a race – it is a relay.
column6: You take the baton, run your lap, then hand it to the next generation.

column5: Every degree is a map of what we know – but the edges are where discovery lives.
column6: Do not fear the blank spaces; draw on them.

column5: A library is a hospital for the ignorant mind.
column6: Check in often – the cure is free.

column5: The school bell rings for the last time, but the learning bell never stops.
column6: When you hear it, smile – and keep listening.

column5: True freedom is the freedom to learn anything, anywhere, anytime.
column6: Protect that freedom as you protect your own breath.

column5: The internet is the greatest university ever built – and it has no walls.
column6: Use it to learn, not just to scroll.

column5: Every mistake is a broken pencil – sharpen it and keep writing.
column6: Do not throw away your work because of one wrong line.

column5: Every child is an unwritten book – the teacher is just the first reader.
column6: Read them carefully – the story is still being written.

column5: A dictionary is the only place where success comes before work.
column6: In real life, work comes first – but the dictionary is a good reminder of the goal.

column5: The most powerful question in any classroom is 'I don’t understand – can you explain again?'
column6: Asking it is not weakness – it is the beginning of wisdom.

column5: When you teach a child to dream, you give them wings.
column6: But you also teach them how to land safely – that is the real gift.

column5: A fresh notebook is a promise: I will fill you with things that matter.
column6: Keep that promise every day.

column5: Mistakes are not failures – they are eraser marks on the paper of learning.
column6: A clean page never taught anyone anything.

column5: Education is the door that opens to every other door.
column6: Without it, you are knocking on walls.

column5: A teacher’s chalk is a baton in a relay race that never ends.
column6: Pass it gently – the next runner is watching.

column5: In a library, silence is not emptiness – it is the sound of a thousand minds thinking.
column6: Listen – you can almost hear the synapses firing.

column5: Education is the only investment whose interest compounds for generations.
column6: Plant that tree of knowledge; your grandchildren will sit in its shade.

column5: The most important lessons are never graded – kindness, honesty, courage.
column6: But they are the only ones that matter at the end.

column5: A classroom where no one asks questions is a cemetery of curiosity.
column6: Dig up the questions – let them breathe.

column5: Life is an open‑book exam – but the book is your memory.
column6: Fill it well while you can.

column5: The hardest teacher is life – but she also gives the longest lesson.
column6: Do not drop out early.

column5: Knowledge is the only thing that becomes yours even if you borrowed it.
column6: So borrow freely – and then lend.

column5: Every graduation is not an ending – it is the end of one paragraph and the beginning of another.
column6: Keep writing.

column5: The real school teaches you how to be human – not just how to earn a wage.
column6: Do not skip those classes.

column5: Tradition is the compass, not the destination.
column6: Let it point you, but walk your own path.

column5: Every lesson you learn becomes a memory – and memories are the only things you can never lose.
column6: So learn richly.

column5: Every language you learn is a new pair of eyes.
column6: Suddenly you see colours you never knew existed.

column5: Curiosity is the window – throw it wide.
column6: The wind of knowledge will rush in.

column5: One educated girl can change a village.
column6: One village can change a country. One country can change the world.

column5: Education is not a sprint – it is a long walk on a worn path.
column6: Enjoy the walk; the destination is not the point.

column5: A candle loses nothing by lighting another – and a mind loses nothing by teaching another.
column6: Light as many as you can.

column5: A life without learning is a symphony without the last movement.
column6: Keep playing the notes – the music is never finished.

column5: Knowledge given away returns to you multiplied.
column6: Be generous with what you know.

column5: The best students are the ones who ask the questions no one else dares to ask.
column6: Be that student.

column5: Every moment is a classroom – do not waste your tuition.
column6: Pay attention.

column5: Every story you read is a life you get to live without dying.
column6: Live many lives – read widely.

column5: The past is a teacher – listen to her, but do not live in her classroom forever.
column6: Learn, then move forward.

column5: Education is the road map – but you have to walk the road yourself.
column6: Take the first step today.

column5: The school bell may ring, but the learning bell never stops.
column6: Keep your ears open.

column5: At the desk of life, everyone is a student.
column6: No one has graduated yet.

column5: Sometimes the most important lesson is to break the rules – but only after you understand them.
column6: Rebel wisely.

column5: The only limit to your learning is the sky – and the sky is not the limit, it is the beginning.
column6: Look up and learn.

column5: A well‑read book is a well‑loved friend – its worn pages are medals of honour.
column6: Wear them proudly.

column5: Every time you teach, you breathe life into someone else’s lungs.
column6: Breathe generously.

column5: The most beautiful thing in the world is an unanswered question – because it means you are still curious.
column6: Stay curious.

column5: Education is the door – walk through it.
column6: On the other side is a world without walls.

column5: Every morning is the first page of a new book – write it well.
column6: Do not skip the preface.

column5: A single candle can light a thousand others without dimming.
column6: Your knowledge is that candle – share it.

column5: The last lesson of the year is always the same: you never stop learning.
column6: See you next year – same school, new lessons.

column5: As the year ends, remember: every day was a page in your textbook of life.
column6: Read it again – and learn from every page.
